hypothesis: Current error and the printed error-squared heuristic do not generally rank probes by true expected regret reduction under irreducible noise or model misspecification.

controls.description: All probe outcomes are enumerated exactly, so oracle EVSI and true expected regret reduction do not depend on Monte Carlo error.
A high-current-error regime makes every candidate probe independent of the latent state.
A learner-assumed high-information signal is uninformative under the true data-generating process.

gates.criterion: In the learnable regime, mutual information correlates at least 0.7 with oracle EVSI and the true signal ranks first.
In the irreducible-noise regime, current error remains high while oracle EVSI is zero for every probe.
Under misspecification, the highest-information assumed probe differs from the oracle choice and has negative true expected regret reduction.
